Wings & Wishes
Transporting critically ill children since 2006
Feather Force — Monthly Giving

There are children in South Africa who can't get life-saving treatment. Because they can't afford to get there.

Your monthly gift changes that.

Wings & Wishes covers the full cost of travel — flights, buses, transfers — for critically and chronically ill children and a parent, so they can reach specialist care that isn't available where they live.

I didn't know how I was going to manage this — I've been so exhausted and burdened with loans. Good people, you have truly made my day.

The problem

The treatment exists. Getting there is the barrier.

When a child is diagnosed with cancer, organ failure, or a life-threatening condition, a family's world collapses. But for many families across South Africa, the heartbreak isn't only the diagnosis.

The specialists are waiting. The hospitals are ready. But there's no money for the flight, the bus, or the R120 transfer from the station to the ward.

A flight to Cape Town. A coach ticket that costs more than a week's groceries. The taxi from the airport. These journeys happen four times a year, for years on end. Some children spend six months in hospital, far from home.

Without a way to get there, they don't get treated. Without treatment, they die.

What Wings & Wishes does

We make sure the journey is never the reason a child misses their treatment.

Wings & Wishes covers flights, coach tickets, and transfers door to door — for the child and a parent. Because no child should face this alone, and no parent should have to choose between being there and having enough money to get there.

These trips happen up to four times a year, for up to five years. We handle every booking, every logistic, every detail — so families can focus on what matters.

We save lives one trip at a time. If we are not involved — these children would die.
Inge Human, Manager, Wings & Wishes
How we work

From referral to hospital door — we handle everything.

From the moment a doctor refers a child, we manage every detail — flights, coach tickets, transfers, hospital coordination. Any child who needs to travel more than 150km to reach specialist care qualifies, including at Red Cross Children's, Groote Schuur and Tygerberg in Cape Town; and Charlotte Maxeke, Nelson Mandela Children's Hospital and Wits Donald Gordon in Johannesburg.

Flights
Domestic air travel to specialist hospitals
Coach tickets
Intercape & long-distance travel across SA
Transfers
Airport & station to hospital door
Buddy Bags
Essentials packed for every child
We don't just book the ticket. Every child travels with a Buddy Bag — pyjamas, a soft toy, toiletries — packed by people who know this journey is hard enough already.
Stories

These children made it. Because people like you said "yes, I care."

Denay is thriving.
Burns recovery · Arniston → Cape Town
At one year old, Denay suffered severe burns and needed weekly visits to Red Cross Children's Hospital — with no car, no public transport, and no way to get there. Six years on, she is strong, confident, and smiling.
Inganathi is 8 and full of life.
Rare condition · Eastern Cape → Cape Town
At two weeks old, Inganathi's parents rushed her to hospital. The diagnosis was extraordinary — and the surgery, high-risk. Wings & Wishes flew her father to Cape Town so she didn't face it alone.
Emetle got the call. She made it.
Liver transplant · Maclear → Johannesburg
From seven months old, Emetle's mother made the long journey from rural Maclear to Johannesburg, month after month. On 5 February 2025, a liver became available. They arrived in time. The transplant was successful.
In their own words
Wings and Wishes, thank you so much for your massive part of our journey. For our flight up, for funding our family to visit, for our flight back home. Your work is exceptional.

The specialists who treat these children rely on us.

Dr. Marisa Beretta
Paediatric Hepatologist
Wits University Donald Gordon Medical Centre
In a country where geography, logistics, and finances stand between a patient and their second chance at life, Wings and Wishes steps in — with compassion, coordination, and unwavering commitment. Their support has made a tangible difference in the lives of our patients, and we cannot thank them enough.
Letitia Jordaan
Regional Coordinator
Smile Foundation, Free State
Without Wings & Wishes, a lot of children would still be waiting for the medical care they needed. We have proof of so many successful stories — because of them. You always walk that extra mile for us.
Five major children's charities — CHOC, the Smile Foundation, Paedspal, the Hero Burns Foundation and the Pelo Foundation — rely on Wings & Wishes to move their patients.
Paedspal Hero Burns Foundation Pelo Foundation

Feather Force — monthly giving

Cover the cost of a child's journey to treatment.

The Feather Force is our community of monthly donors — people who show up every month for children who have no other way to get there.

Why monthly? Cassidy flew with us 33 times before she even made the liver transplant list. Her 42nd trip was the one that saved her life. These children don't need one trip — they need every trip. That's what your monthly gift guarantees.

The Spark
R99
per month
The transfer from the coach station to the hospital door.
A single transfer costs R120. You're most of the way there.
Most popular
The Journey
R249
per month
An airport transfer — from the terminal to the ward.
Airport transfers cost R350. You cover most of it, every month.
The Ride
R499
per month
A full coach ticket for one child's treatment trip.
A coach ticket costs R850. Your gift covers more than half.
Takes 2 minutes · Cancel anytime · Section 18A tax deductible
FAQ

Everything you might want to know.

Is Wings & Wishes a registered NPO?
Yes. Registered in South Africa since 2006. Your donation qualifies for a Section 18A tax deduction. [NPO number to be confirmed]
Does 100% of my donation go to transport?
Every rand goes directly to travel costs. Our website is run by a volunteer, and our operations are lean by design — so when you give, it moves. Straight to a flight, a coach ticket, or a transfer to the hospital door.
What conditions do the children have?
Any child travelling more than 150km for specialist care qualifies — cancer, heart conditions, organ transplants, retinoblastoma, rare diseases, bone marrow conditions. Any illness, any child. If the treatment isn't available near home, we help them reach it.
Won't the government cover this?
Families must first apply for state hospital transport and prove it isn't available before we step in. We are the safety net for when the system can't help — and for these 600 children, it can't.
How do I know my money is well managed?
Wings & Wishes is audited annually by an independent external firm, with monthly trustee oversight. Every trip is documented with full evidence and progress reports. We know and document the personal story of every one of our 600 children — no child is a number to us.
What if I need to cancel?
Cancel anytime, no questions asked. Manage it directly through PayFast or email Inge at inge.h@wingsandwishes.org.
One more story

Awonke made it because someone covered the cost of his flight.

On 23 August 2022, a donor heart became available. He had eight hours to reach Cape Town. He had already missed two donor hearts — because his family couldn't afford the journey.

Wings & Wishes was ready. He boarded the last flight from Bloemfontein. He landed at 19:30. The transplant was done at 23:00. He was home by December. He is now a teenager living a normal life.

Be that person for the next child.
